policy_title: Lower Extremity Major Joint Replacement (Hip and Knee)
scope: Total Hip Arthroplasty (THA) and Total Knee Arthroplasty (TKA)

total_knee_arthroplasty.threshold: 3 or more of the following criteria must be met

criteria.label: Advanced Joint Disease (Imaging)
criteria.description: Advanced joint disease demonstrated by radiographic or MRI evidence

criteria.label: Failed Conservative Therapy
criteria.description: History of unsuccessful appropriate conservative therapy clearly documented in pre-procedure medical record
conservative_treatments: Flexibility and muscle strengthening exercises with supervised physical therapy

criteria.label: Pain with Functional Disability
criteria.description: Pain with functional disability due to arthritis or trauma to the knee joint; ADLs diminished despite compliance with plan of care
plan_of_care_elements: Therapeutic injections into the knee as appropriate

criteria.label: Distinct Structural Abnormalities
qualifying_conditions: Distal femur fracture
Proximal tibia fracture
Malignancy of the distal femur, proximal tibia, knee joint or adjacent soft tissues
Avascular or other form of osteonecrosis of the knee
Rheumatologic changes precluding or inconsistent with rehabilitation

criteria.label: Failed Previous Joint Replacement / Revision
criteria.description: Failed previous joint replacement/arthroplasty necessitating revision
qualifying_conditions: Loosening, fracture, or mechanical failure of one or more components
Technical or functional failure of previous knee surgery (e.g., unicompartmental knee replacement)
Previous osteotomy or partial arthroplasty
Infection
Periprosthetic fracture or bone loss of distal femur, proximal tibia or patella
Implant or knee malalignment
Bearing surface wear leading to symptomatic synovitis
Tibiofemoral or extensor mechanism instability
Knee stiffness, arthrofibrosis or other destructive conditions precluding employment or functional activities

total_hip_arthroplasty.threshold: 3 or more of the following indications must be met

criteria.label: Advanced Joint Disease (Imaging)
criteria.description: Advanced joint disease demonstrated by radiographic or MRI evidence

criteria.label: Pain and Functional Disability
criteria.description: Pain and functional disability from injury due to trauma or arthritis; ADLs diminished despite completing a plan of care
plan_of_care_elements: Flexibility and muscle strengthening exercises with supervised physical therapy

criteria.label: Failed Conservative Treatment
criteria.description: History of unsuccessful conservative treatment or non-surgical medical management clearly documented in pre-procedure medical record

criteria.label: Distinct Structural Abnormalities
qualifying_conditions: Malignancy of the joint involving bones or soft tissues of the pelvis or proximal femur
Avascular necrosis (osteonecrosis of femoral head)
Fracture of the femoral neck
Acetabular fracture
Non-union or failure of previous hip fracture surgery
Mal-union of acetabular or proximal femur fracture

criteria.label: Failed Previous Hip Arthroplasty / Revision
criteria.description: Failed previous Hip Arthroplasty necessitating revision
qualifying_conditions: Loosening, fracture or mechanical failure of the implant
Instability of one or more components
Recurrent or irreducible dislocation
Infection
Displaced periprosthetic fracture
Clinically significant leg length inequality
Progressive soft tissue or bone reaction or substantial bone loss
Clinically significant audible noise
Bearing surface wear leading to symptomatic synovitis
Other disease or destructive process precluding employment or functional activities

contraindications.description: Procedures are NOT medically reasonable and necessary when any of the following are present
contraindications.logic: ANY of the following present → DENY
conditions: Active infection of the hip or knee joint or active systemic bacteremia
Active skin infection or open wound within the planned surgical site
Progressive neurological disease etiologic for pain, instability or disability

limitations: TKA and THA not considered reasonable and necessary when covered indications are not met

provider_qualifications.board_certification: Certified or eligible for certification by the American Board of Orthopaedic Surgery
training_pathways: Accredited residency or fellowship program in the applicable specialty/subspecialty
Extensive CME activities — comprehensive courses offered/sponsored/endorsed by a US academic institution or applicable specialty society, designated AMA Category 1 Credit

general: All documentation maintained in patient medical record and available to contractor upon request
Every page legible with appropriate patient identification and dates of service
Legible signature of responsible physician or non-physician practitioner
Documentation must support medical necessity as directed by this policy
Must support CMS signature requirements (Pub. 100-08, Chapter 3)

for_advanced_joint_disease.logic: ALL of the following must be documented
required_elements: Arthritis of knee or hip supported by X-ray or MRI showing qualifying findings (subchondral cysts, subchondral sclerosis, periarticular osteophytes, joint subluxation, joint space narrowing, avascular necrosis, or bone on bone articulations)
Pain with functional disability at hip or knee (e.g., pain interfering with ADLs, pain increased with activity initiation, pain with weight bearing, pain precluding sleep)
History of reasonable attempt (typically 3+ months) at conservative therapy (e.g., NSAID trial or documented contraindication, supervised physical therapy)
Documentation that ADLs are diminished despite non-surgical management
Risk/benefit of surgery appropriately addressed for patients with significant comorbidities
Supporting evidence (e.g., pathology reports, oncologist referral for malignancy, X-ray for fracture)
Documentation of functional disability when pain is a reason for procedure
Risk/benefit addressed for patients with significant comorbidities
Lab or pathology reports when infection is the reason for revision surgery, plus documentation of infection treatment and physician note supporting appropriateness of surgery

hospital_record_requirements: History and physical describing present issue, duration, current signs/symptoms, and comorbidities
Physician progress notes with physical exam findings (deformity, ROM abnormalities, crepitus, effusion, tenderness, gait abnormalities)
Any other relevant information addressing coverage criteria related to the episode of care prior to hospitalization
documentation_requirements.bilateral_procedures: All criteria apply to bilateral surgery; medical record must also support medical necessity for performing THA or TKA bilaterally

utilization_guidelines.standard_of_practice: Consistent with locally acceptable standards of practice (CMS Ruling 95-1)
utilization_guidelines.review_triggers: Services performed in excess of peer norms based on data analysis may be subject to prepay or post-pay medical review
utilization_guidelines.device_requirements: Devices/implants must be FDA-regulated Class II or Class III devices meeting CFR 21, Chapter 1, Subchapter H, Part 888

decision_logic_summary.approve: At least 3 of the 5 covered indication criteria are met AND no contraindications are present AND provider qualifications are met AND documentation requirements are satisfied
decision_logic_summary.deny: Fewer than 3 criteria are met OR any contraindication is present OR documentation is insufficient

related_documents.billing_and_coding_article: A56796 — Billing and Coding: Lower Extremity Major Joint Replacement (Hip and Knee)
